intro: Crepis tsarongensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to the alpine regions of the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepis tsarongensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Crepis tsarongensis is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
